title: 10 best herbal teas for shortness of breath

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath by improving respiratory function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in purple coneflower tea, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, may help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant properties: Purple coneflower tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing shortness of breath caused by bronchitis, pneumonia, or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with shortness of breath because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce inflammation in the airways and improve lung function. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy respiratory function, Echinacea purpurea teas may help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath and promote overall respiratory well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, including echinacoside and isobutylamides, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby easing shortness of breath.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives: These phenolic compounds, such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.

Alkylamides: These compounds, including isobutylamides and isovaleryl amide, have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that may help relax the airway muscles and improve lung function, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which are believed to help alleviate respiratory issues.
Roots: They are used because they have been traditionally used to treat various respiratory problems, including shortness of breath, due to their adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: They are used because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes and strain it into a cup.
Enjoy the tea immediately while it is still warm for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe by decreasing swelling and congestion.
Flavonoids: Act as a natural bronchodilator, relaxing the airway muscles and increasing airflow, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to breathing difficulties.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with shortness of breath because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the airways. The roots of this plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the airway muscles. This can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen intake. As a result, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may provide relief from shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra tea help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath.

Fruits: Fruits are used because they contain glycosides and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 medium-sized roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Clean the roots thoroughly with a fine mesh strainer and rinse with cool water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 6-8 hours.
Grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of powdered root in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make the tea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 fresh leaves and flowers of taraxacum officinale in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Clean the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of the cleaned taraxacum officinale in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves and flowers.
Serve the taraxacum officinale tea immediately and let it cool to a comfortable temperature for drinking.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 30 grams of eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves from a healthy plant.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a dark place for 2 weeks at room temperature.
Crush 2 teaspoons of dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of powdered eupatorium perfoliatum in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then drink the remaining 250 milliliters of tea immediately.

Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately, while still warm, in a dose of 1/2 cup three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with shortness of breath because its calming properties can relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constricting bronchial tubes. The tea's antispasmodic effects also help to ease muscle spasms in the lungs and diaphragm, allowing for smoother breathing. Additionally, lavender's natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds may help to combat underlying respiratory infections or allergies that contribute to shortness of breath. By promoting relaxation and reducing airway constriction, English lavender teas can provide welcome relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating shortness of breath caused by conditions like asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
Calming and sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender can help relax the respiratory muscles, reducing bronchospasm and improving airflow to alleviate shortness of breath.
Antioxidant activity: Lavender tea's antioxidants may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to lung tissues, which can contribute to chronic breathing difficulties and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have a calming effect on the respiratory system, making them an excellent choice for relieving symptoms of shortness of breath.
Buds: The buds of the lavender plant contain high concentrations of essential oils, which help to soothe and relax the airways, alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried lavender flowers from a trusted source for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the harvested lavender flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and excess botanicals.
Add one teaspoon of honey if desired for flavor and potential additional benefits before serving.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional two minutes before serving hot or chilled as preferred.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains a compound called anethole, which has natural expectorant properties. When brewed into a tea, anise can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, anise's antispasmodic effects can help relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, allowing for smoother airflow and reducing wheezing and coughing. Overall, anise teas can provide relief from shortness of breath by opening up the airways and improving lung function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Anise tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe by promoting effective coughing and expectoration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, making it effective in relieving congestion and coughs associated with shortness of breath.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, particularly anethole, which has been traditionally used to soothe bronchial passages and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried pimpinella anisum flowers and leaves into a small muslin bag or teaball.
Heat filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat until it reaches a rolling boil.
Pour the boiling water over the pimpinella anisum herbs in the muslin bag, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the muslin bag or teaball, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, enjoying the soothing effects on shortness of breath within 30-60 minutes after consumption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbs such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have been used to ease congestion and inflammation in the lungs. Other herbs like ginkgo biloba and hawthorn may help improve circulation and oxygenation of the body, reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for shortness of breath are a natural remedy that helps alleviate respiratory issues by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the airways. The best herbal teas for shortness of breath include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Ephedra sinica, Verbascum thapsus, Thymus vulgaris, and Astragalus membranaceus. If left untreated, shortness of breath can severely impact daily life, limiting physical activity, causing anxiety, and affecting sleep quality, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with shortness of breath is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Licorice root tea. Peppermint tea helps to relax the airways and reduce inflammation, while Ginger tea aids in circulation and warming the body. Licorice root tea soothes the throat and mucous membranes, reducing congestion. Together, these teas work to alleviate shortness of breath by calming the respiratory system, opening airways, and reducing inflammation, promoting easier breathing and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with shortness of breath because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and congestion in the airways. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea also exhibit antioxidant activity, protecting the lungs from oxidative stress and promoting overall respiratory health. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the immune response and alleviate respiratory issues caused by infections or allergies, thereby easing shortness of breath and improving lung function. This leads to better oxygenation and breathing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate shortness of breath.
Phenolic acids: They possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage, thereby reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
Alkylphenols: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may contribute to inflammation and shortness of breath in the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make teas for shortness of breath because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote respiratory health.
Leaves: They are used to make teas for shortness of breath because they have expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Flowers: They are used to make teas for shortness of breath because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help soothe and calm the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powdered echinacea purpurea with 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve immediately while the flavors and medicinal properties are at their peak potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with shortness of breath because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax airway muscles. The active compound, gingerol, in Zingiber officinale tea has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can exacerbate respiratory issues. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the airways, Zingiber officinale tea can help improve lung function and increase oxygen flow, providing relief from shortness of breath and making it easier to breathe. This can be especially beneficial for individuals with respiratory conditions such as asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, helping to expand the airways and improve airflow, which in turn reduces shortness of breath.

Volatile oils (including Gingerol's dehydro analogue): The volatile oils in Zingiber officinale possess bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax the airway muscles and improve airflow, providing relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Buds: They are often used in teas to help ease congestion and promote easy breathing due to their volatile oils and antioxidants.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water for steeping.
Rinse the dried zingiber officinale root under cold running water for 10 seconds.
Combine the rinsed zingiber officinale root with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the zingiber officinale root to steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with shortness of breath because it improves blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have vasodilatory properties, which widen blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. This increased circulation helps to alleviate shortness of breath by allowing more oxygen to reach the lungs and other organs. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to its beneficial effects on respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, thereby improving breathing.

Iridoids (specifically Ginkgotoxin): Although not fully understood, these compounds may help to relax blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the lungs and alleviate shortness of breath symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba in teas for shortness of breath due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Buds: Buds are also used in teas to alleviate shortness of breath as they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids than mature leaves.
(Optional) Seeds: Seeds are sometimes used in teas for shortness of breath due to their content of flavonoids and terpenoids, although they are less commonly used than leaves and buds.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the liquid from the leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea if desired for a more palatable flavor.
Allow the tea to cool before serving and consuming to avoid scalding your mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the respiratory system. The flavonoids and saponins present in the tea can also help to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties can help to clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of shortness of breath. This can provide relief for those suffering from respiratory conditions.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps alleviate shortness of breath by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs.
Liquiritigenin: A phenolic compound, liquiritigenin helps ease shortness of breath by relaxing airway muscles and reducing bronchial constriction.
18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid: A triterpenoid acid, 18α-glycyrrhetinic acid helps alleviate shortness of breath by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and improving lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in teas for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Barks: The barks are used in some teas for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the airway muscles and reduce shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and a tea infuser or strainer.
Combine the dried roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to fully infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or strainer.
Consume the tea within 30 minutes of preparation while the flavors are still potent.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with shortness of breath because its calming properties can ease respiratory issues. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the airways and improve breathing. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress, a common contributor to respiratory problems. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia tea may provide relief from shortness of breath and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to open up airways and improve respiratory function.
Caryophyllene: Has anti-inflammatory effects that may help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used to make teas for shortness of breath as they contain volatile oils that can help calm the respiratory system.

Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used to make teas for shortness of breath as they are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the respiratory system.
Buds: Lavandula angustifolia buds are used to make teas for shortness of breath as they contain essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making breathing easier.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Bring 250 milliliters of water to a boil in a medium saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and add the dried flowers to the boiling water for 5-7 minutes steeping.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve lined with a cheesecloth for 2 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature for 10-15 minutes before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with shortness of breath because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly eucalyptol and camphor, have a decongestant effect on the airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, rosmarinic acid in the tea helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing constriction and improving airflow. This natural combination of properties makes rosmarinus officinalis tea a soothing and effective remedy for alleviating shortness of breath and promoting respiratory comfort. Its benefits are particularly useful for individuals with asthma and other respiratory conditions.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate shortness of breath by its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing airway inflammation and congestion.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene helps improve shortness of breath by its antioxidant properties, protecting the lungs from oxidative stress and promoting healthy lung function.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene helps with shortness of breath by its bronchodilatory properties, relaxing airway muscles and increasing lung capacity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils that help to ease respiratory issues.
Flowers: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory distress.
Stems: They are a good source of antioxidants and essential oils that may help to relax airways and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves or use 2 teaspoons of dried leaves from the garden.
Rinse the harvested leaves under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a teapot or kettle to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep 1 teaspoon of fresh leaves or 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the leaves before serving immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica teas helps with shortness of breath because of its ability to dilate airways and increase oxygen flow to the lungs. The herbal tea's ephedrine content stimulates the body's natural bronchodilators, relaxing the muscles around the airways and allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, Ephedra sinica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, further improving airflow and alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: It acts as a bronchodilator, which helps in widening the airways and improving breathing by relaxing the smooth muscles in the bronchial tubes.
Isoeugenol: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by making breathing more difficult.
Tylophorine: It has a bronchodilatory effect that helps in widening the airways and improving breathing by relaxing the smooth muscles in the bronchial tubes, similar to ephedrine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their ma huang alkaloids, which help to expand airways.
Stems: Used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their ability to increase oxygen flow and ease breathing.
Leaves: Used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their ma huang content, which helps to dilate bronchial tubes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried ephedra sinica root and 1l of boiling water for the tea base.
Measure out 250ml of water and pour it over the ephedra sinica root in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the ephedra sinica root for 10-15 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool and then drink 100-200ml of it as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains mucilages, which are complex compounds that have soothing properties. These mucilages, particularly verbascoside, help to calm and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. By doing so, it opens up airways, making it easier to breathe and increasing oxygen intake. This natural remedy also has a calming effect on the nervous system, further reducing the feeling of shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: Verbascoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing in individuals with shortness of breath.
Asteroside: Asteroside has bronchodilatory effects, which help relax the airway muscles and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, verbascoside and isoverbascoside): Iridoid glycosides have spasmolytic properties, which help relax the airway muscles and reduce spasms, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion.
Roots: The roots of Verbascum thapsus are used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their diuretic properties, promoting fluid removal and reducing congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their mucolytic properties, breaking down mucus and relieving respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a quantity of dried verbascum thapsus flowers with a total weight of 4-6 ounces.
Cut the dried flowers into smaller pieces using scissors and a surface area of about 12 inches.
Combine the cut verbascum thapsus flowers with 4 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the desired tea strength is achieved.
Strain the tea mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas helps with shortness of breath because it contains thymol, a compound with decongestant properties. Thymol works by breaking down excess mucus in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. The anti-inflammatory properties in thymol also help reduce inflammation in the airways, further alleviating shortness of breath. Additionally, thymus vulgaris teas contain antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, promoting overall respiratory health and improving lung function. This natural remedy can provide relief from respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath, and also exhibits antimicrobial properties to combat infections that may exacerbate respiratory issues.

Bornyl acetate: As a terpene, it helps to relax airway muscles and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe, and its anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to reducing swelling in the airways.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to open up airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are often used to make teas for shortness of breath due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used in teas to alleviate shortness of breath due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used in teas for shortness of breath due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves for making the tea.
Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot for 5 minutes to prepare the hot water.
Steep the dried thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves.
Drink the thymus vulgaris tea immediately after straining to enjoy its flavor and benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas helps with shortness of breath because it is believed to boost the body's energy and immune function. Traditional Chinese medicine views Astragalus as a 'qi tonic,' suggesting it can enhance the flow of 'qi' or vital energy, which is thought to be essential for healthy breathing. By potentially improving lung function and increasing oxygenation, Astragalus membranaceus teas may help alleviate shortness of breath in individuals with respiratory conditions such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) or bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help alleviate shortness of breath by improving cardiovascular function and reducing inflammation in the airways.
Saponins: They aid in reducing shortness of breath by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and improving lung function, particularly in conditions such as bronchitis.
Polysaccharides: They help alleviate shortness of breath by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: A rich source of astragaloside IV, which is believed to help alleviate shortness of breath.
Leaves: Contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributing to their potential bronchodilatory effects.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to relax airway muscles and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus roots and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Boil 8-10 ounces of water in a teapot or a saucepan for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the astragalus membranaceus roots in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes or longer.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Serve the tea hot or at room temperature after the liquid has cooled down slightly.
